name: Saraiki
notes: Saraiki (سرائیکی) of Pakistan, written in the Saraiki Shahmukhi alphabet — an extended Perso-Arabic abjad, with additional letters for the implosive consonants ɓ ɗ ʄ ɠ that are the language's most distinctive feature. The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified. INPUT CONTRACT: as with all Arabic-script languages, short vowels are normally left unwritten, so a correct spec depends either on vowelised (diacritic-bearing) input or on a lexicon; neither is provided here. Metadata only. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode sera1259), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode sera1259.

ancestors.notes: Indo-Aryan — the classification node this language sits under.
